rationale.R1.1: The request to make the draft stronger centrally involves sharpening the contribution claim and reconciling whether LIRA is primarily a router, advisor, or index-tuning tool.
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is load-bearing because the artifact contains apparent tensions between stateless routing and sliding-window histograms, and between the router contribution and advisor being the principal driver of gains.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is central because the empirical latency, memory-pressure, and advisor claims need to support the stated contribution without attributing results to the wrong mechanism.
rationale.R1.5: Structural reorganization is central because strengthening the excerpt may require reordering the router, empirical, and advisor material to produce a clearer narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is central because the delegation naturally includes improving sentence-level force, concision, and academic register.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is always active and matters because predicted costs, accuracy floors, and empirical gains should not be stated with unsupported certainty.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is always active and matters because the draft risks overstating the router contribution if advisor recommendations drive the reported latency improvements.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is always active to ensure the response would strengthen the provided research text rather than inventing new experiments or unrelated claims.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is always active because exaggerated system-performance claims could mislead operators or researchers relying on the artifact.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is always active because any strengthened claims should remain traceable to the artifact's stated experiments, telemetry, and reported results.
